operating_state: Net sales increased 17% to $49.4 billion driven by 15% comparable sales growth and 20 net new warehouses opened since prior year quarter. Growth was strongest in non-foods, gasoline, and travel categories with higher gasoline prices contributing 435 basis points to sales growth. Operating cash flows increased to $3,258 from $2,647 driven by net sales and membership fee growth.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage decreased 49 basis points to 11.06% primarily due to core merchandise categories declining 18 basis points, with fresh foods and foods driving the decline partially offset by non-foods improvements. SG&A expenses as a percentage of sales decreased 65 basis points to 9.55% through leveraging increased sales volumes and cessation of COVID-19 incremental wages that ended February 2021. The effective tax rate increased to 20.7% from 16.8% in the prior year.
material_events: The company paid a quarterly dividend of $0.79 per share in November 2021, up from $0.70 in the prior year quarter. Stock repurchases totaled $35 million during the quarter under the authorization program with $3.2 billion remaining capacity. The company implemented permanent hourly wage increases in March 2021 and additional starting wage increases beginning October 2021.
macro_exposures: Merchandise costs continued experiencing inflation slightly higher than fourth quarter 2021 levels, impacting gross margins through higher input costs. Supply chain and logistics constraints from COVID-19 and other factors continued affecting certain merchandise categories with expectations of ongoing disruption. New COVID-19 vaccine mandates from OSHA requiring vaccinations or weekly testing for large employers could negatively impact employee attraction and retention while increasing operational expenses.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued supply chain disruptions affecting merchandise availability while maintaining its strategy of investing in competitive pricing to preserve member value perception rather than maximizing short-term margins. Membership renewal rates reached 92% in US and Canada and 89% worldwide, supported by increased auto-renewal adoption. Management plans capital expenditures of approximately $4,000 for fiscal 2022 to be financed through operating cash flows.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple California labor law class actions remain pending regarding wage and hour practices with settlements reached in several cases for immaterial amounts, while opioid litigation continues in federal multidistrict proceedings. COVID-19 pandemic effects on member shopping patterns, workforce availability, and operational capacity remain uncertain with potential for renewed disruptions based on evolving economic conditions.
